strands.title: Working directory and filesystem context
strands.content: User 'tony' has established working directory at ~/projects (/home/tony/projects). Local filesystem operations and permission management are available in this context.

strands.title: Local command execution infrastructure
strands.content: System supports local command execution with structured output (stdout, stderr, XML metadata tags). Commands include caveat handling for local operations. Output parsing demonstrates functional command pipeline.

key_claims.claim: Local commands return structured output with stdout/stderr separation

strands.title: Model configuration and selection
strands.content: User invoked /model command and selected 'opus' (claude-opus-4-6). This indicates dynamic model switching capability within the local environment. The command executed successfully with formatted output.

strands.title: User identity and system access
strands.content: User is 'tony' with access to /home/tony/projects and ability to issue commands that configure local system settings (e.g., model selection). Has sufficient permissions to execute configuration commands.

key_claims.claim: User 'tony' has permission to execute /model configuration command

declared_losses.reason: Caveat: Local command outputs (turns 4-6) are explicitly excluded from substantive analysis per user instruction. The fact of model selection is retained; diagnostic details from raw output are not projected forward.
declared_losses.impact: Medium - functional context preserved, diagnostic depth lost

open_questions: What is the user's intent with the ~/projects directory and model configuration?
Will the model selection (opus) affect subsequent reasoning or response generation?
Is this model selection a one-time configuration or persistent for the session?
What follow-up commands or queries will the user issue with this configuration in place?
